title: Board Updates Dashboard
excerpt: I’ve found that the Board Updates (where there is the chat/update function at the very top of a board next to the name, info, and favorite star) is a perfect spot for me to store overarching notes, reminders, etc. that c…

posts.author: JessMergen (Jessica Mergen)
posts.cookedHtml:
I’ve found that the Board Updates (where there is the chat/update function at the very top of a board next to the name, info, and favorite star) is a perfect spot for me to store overarching notes, reminders, etc. that come in through channels like verbal, chat, e-mail, etc. but don’t belong to any one task. By putting them in board updates, I then do not need to hunt through task updates for info. I could put these notes anywhere, but I would rather keep them “in Board” rather than in a separate tool (ex. OneNote), or in a separate WorkDoc for each project.

I would like to create a Dashboard or Mirror, or Workdoc with a widget for these Board Updates so I can see them all on one page - to view and add-to/update to them throughout my week. Filtering the Board Updates dashboard widget would help, but a more full featured function would also enable these updates to form a method of reporting up to leadership.

posts.author: Iryna_Belaya (Iryna Belaya)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hello @JessMergen ,
Alternatively, you can try the Board Email Reports app that has a set of reports dedicated to item updates:

  • Updates by a specific employee
  • Updates mentioning a specific employee
  • Updates containing attachments
  • Status updates
  • Last updates
    The app consolidates item updates over a specific period and automatically delivers them via email with a roundup of changes and detailed xls-report as an attachment. This is how it looks:
    image424×469 24.9 KB

    image1462×439 83.1 KB

You can test the app for free and find out if it works for you.
